meta.title: Annual ESG Occupational Health & Safety Disclosure — FY2025
meta.subtitle: GRI 403 / SASB / ESRS S1 — own-workforce + non-employee · {ORG} · FY2025
meta.author: Head of Sustainability / HSE
meta.classification: For assurance & publication — decision-support; NOT a final assured or audited disclosure. An ESG / Sustainability Assurance Specialist and a competent person must review before publication.
meta.deid_notice: All OH&S figures are aggregated to the reporting boundary by category only — no individual incident, injured party, exact date or location is identifiable. Any injury/illness category of fewer than 5 individuals is suppressed, and secondary suppression is applied so no suppressed cell can be back-calculated from a published total or the remaining cells. No re-identification key appears in this document.

sections.text: This disclosure covers occupational health & safety for {ORG} for FY2025 (1 Jan–31 Dec 2025). Reporting boundary: operational control. Workforce coverage is reported for own-workforce AND non-employee (contractor) populations separately, as required by ESRS S1; each rate is computed on that population's own hours-worked denominator. Intended assurance level: LIMITED (negative conclusion). Materiality: own-workforce and contractor H&S is assessed as material on a double-materiality basis (impact on workers + financial). Every figure below carries its boundary, denominator and source; a figure with no stated boundary/denominator is not assurable and is not published.

Decision-support / pre-assurance draft. This is the ISO 45001 clause 9.1 performance evaluation prepared for external disclosure; it precedes — and never replaces — the assurance engagement and the competent-person review. It does not constitute a limited or reasonable assurance opinion.

Rates are computed deterministically by the incident_rates engine from recordable counts and hours worked, to standard definitions (BLS/OSHA recordkeeping); they are not estimated in prose. Each population carries its own denominator. Fatalities and ill-health are aggregated and small-cell suppressed (see the de-identification notice).

sections.caption: Every figure carries population, denominator and definition. Lagging rates from incident_rates (anchors TRIR 2.07 / LTIFR 6.00); a rate with no denominator/definition is refused, not published.

sections.text: Small-cell control: where an injury/illness category for a single site or demographic falls below 5, it is suppressed AND a second cell is suppressed/aggregated so the suppressed value cannot be recovered as (total minus the other published cells). No published table permits back-calculation of a <5 cell.

rows: Hazard ID, risk assessment, incident investigation
Workers covered by the OH&S management system
sections.caption: Disclosure code to location, selected from the reused KB-STD-ESG-GRI403 crosswalk (no duplicate index minted). A claimed-but-absent required disclosure is recorded [GAP], never papered over — fabricating a missing disclosure is a citation hard-fail.

items: Each metric carries definition + denominator + source + period + completeness/accuracy note (KB-SNIP-ESG-ASSURANCE section 3).
Reporting boundary (operational control) and the own-workforce vs non-employee split are stated for every figure (ESRS S1 mandate).
Intended assurance level (LIMITED) is stated so evidence rigour matches it; double-materiality basis is stated.
Lagging rates are engine-computed (incident_rates), not prose estimates; comparators carry source + year or [GAP].

sections.text: Forward commitments (hierarchy of controls; owned + dated)

rows.action: Extend recordable-injury hours-worked capture to all contractor populations so every contractor rate carries its own denominator (closing the 403-3 [GAP]).
Implement engineered controls for the period's leading injury mechanism ahead of any PPE/admin-only measure (hierarchy of controls).

sections.text: These are forward disclosure/programme commitments tied to the ISO 45001 9.1 performance evaluation; each is owned (role label) and dated. This disclosure is decision-support for assurance and publication and does not assert that the figures have been assured or audited.
